requirements.title: VSCode Extension
requirements.description: The LibJuno VSCode extension shall be a VSCode extension that assists developers in navigating LibJuno-based projects.
requirements.rationale: VSCode is one of the most widely adopted development environments, making it the highest-impact platform for LibJuno developer tooling.

requirements.title: Vtable-Aware Go to Definition
requirements.description: The extension shall provide Go to Definition navigation that resolves vtable-dispatched API function calls to their concrete implementation functions.
requirements.rationale: Users have requested the ability to navigate directly from vtable-dispatched API calls to their concrete implementations.

requirements.title: LibJuno API Pattern Recognition
requirements.description: The extension shall recognize the LibJuno vtable API pattern (function pointer fields accessed via API struct pointers).
requirements.rationale: LibJuno's vtable-based dependency injection pattern introduces a level of indirection that standard IDE tooling cannot resolve, making it difficult for developers to trace API calls to their concrete implementations.

requirements.title: Graceful Error on Missing Implementation
requirements.description: The extension shall report an error to the user when no concrete implementation can be found for a vtable API function call.
requirements.rationale: A concrete implementation may not be resolvable in all cases, such as when the vtable assignment has not yet been written. The extension must handle this gracefully to avoid confusing or misleading the developer.

requirements.title: Single Implementation Navigation
requirements.description: When exactly one concrete implementation is found for a vtable API function call, the extension shall navigate directly to the line of that function's definition, not to the vtable assignment, the vtable initializer entry, or a forward declaration of that function.
requirements.rationale: When there is a single unambiguous implementation, direct navigation to the definition line minimizes friction and matches the behavior developers expect from Go to Definition. Navigating to the vtable initializer or a forward declaration forces the developer to perform a second navigation step, defeating the purpose of the feature.

requirements.title: Multiple Implementation Selection
requirements.description: When multiple concrete implementations are found for a vtable API function call, the extension shall present a selection list to the user.
requirements.rationale: Multiple implementations may exist across a workspace. Presenting a selection list allows the developer to choose the correct target without the extension making an incorrect assumption.

requirements.title: Native Go to Definition Integration
requirements.description: The extension shall integrate with VSCode's built-in Go to Definition system.
requirements.rationale: Integrating with the native Go to Definition system (Ctrl+Click, F12) provides a familiar, zero-learning-curve experience for developers.

requirements.title: Module Root API Discovery
requirements.description: The extension shall parse JUNO_MODULE_ROOT macro usage to identify the API struct type associated with a module root.
requirements.rationale: JUNO_MODULE_ROOT is the canonical macro that associates a module root struct with its API type. Using it as the source of truth ensures the extension is consistent with the module system and does not rely on naming conventions that may vary.

requirements.title: Module Derivation Chain Resolution
requirements.description: The extension shall parse JUNO_MODULE_DERIVE macro usage to resolve the derivation hierarchy and determine the applicable API type for derived modules.
requirements.rationale: Derived modules inherit their root's API type through JUNO_MODULE_DERIVE. The extension must walk this chain to correctly associate a derived type with its API, since most user code operates on derived types rather than roots.

requirements.title: Designated Initializer Recognition
requirements.description: The extension shall recognize vtable function assignments using C designated initializer syntax.
requirements.rationale: Designated initializers are the most common and recommended syntax for populating vtable structs in LibJuno projects.

requirements.title: Direct Assignment Recognition
requirements.description: The extension shall recognize vtable function assignments using direct assignment syntax.
requirements.rationale: Some developers or legacy code may assign vtable fields individually after declaration. The extension should support this pattern to avoid false negatives.

requirements.title: Positional Initializer Recognition
requirements.description: The extension shall recognize vtable function assignments using non-designated positional initializer syntax.
requirements.rationale: Positional initialization without field designators is valid C and may be used in some codebases. Supporting it ensures the extension works regardless of initialization style.

requirements.title: Informative Non-Intrusive Error
requirements.description: The extension shall present resolution errors in a manner that is informative but non-intrusive to the user's workflow.
requirements.rationale: Resolution failures are expected during normal development. Intrusive error dialogs would disrupt the developer's workflow and train them to ignore extension feedback.

requirements.title: Trait Root API Discovery
requirements.description: The extension shall parse JUNO_TRAIT_ROOT macro usage to identify the API struct type associated with a trait root.
requirements.rationale: JUNO_TRAIT_ROOT is the canonical macro that associates a trait root struct with its API type. Using it as the source of truth ensures the extension is consistent with the trait system and does not rely on naming conventions that may vary.

requirements.title: Trait Derivation Chain Resolution
requirements.description: The extension shall parse JUNO_TRAIT_DERIVE macro usage to resolve the derivation hierarchy and determine the applicable API type for derived traits.
requirements.rationale: Derived traits inherit their root's API type through JUNO_TRAIT_DERIVE. The extension must walk this chain to correctly associate a derived type with its API, since most user code operates on derived types rather than roots.

requirements.title: Failure Handler Navigation
requirements.description: The extension shall provide Go to Definition navigation that resolves failure handler function pointers to their concrete implementation functions.
requirements.rationale: Failure handlers are injected via dependency injection in the same manner as vtable API functions. Developers need to trace failure handler assignments to understand diagnostic and error-handling behavior in their system.

requirements.title: AI Agent Accessibility
requirements.description: The extension shall be accessible by various AI agent platforms.
requirements.rationale: AI agents are increasingly used alongside human developers for code navigation, analysis, and generation tasks. The extension must be accessible to both human and AI developers to maximize its utility and ensure that AI-assisted workflows benefit from the same vtable resolution capabilities as manual workflows.

requirements.title: AI Vtable Resolution Access
requirements.description: The extension shall expose vtable API function resolution capabilities to AI developer tools.
requirements.rationale: AI developer tools need to resolve vtable-dispatched API calls to concrete implementations in order to understand and reason about LibJuno codebases effectively.

requirements.title: AI Failure Handler Resolution Access
requirements.description: The extension shall expose failure handler function resolution capabilities to AI developer tools.
requirements.rationale: AI developer tools need to trace failure handler assignments to understand error-handling behavior when analyzing or generating LibJuno code.

requirements.title: Platform-Agnostic AI Interface
requirements.description: The extension shall expose its capabilities to AI developer tools through a mechanism that is not specific to any single AI agent platform.
requirements.rationale: The AI tooling ecosystem is diverse and evolving. A platform-agnostic interface ensures the extension remains useful regardless of which AI tools a developer chooses to use.

requirements.title: C and C++ File Type Support
requirements.description: The extension shall discover and index source files with common C and C++ file extensions, including at minimum: .c, .h, .cpp, .hpp, .hh, and .cc.
requirements.rationale: LibJuno-based projects may contain both C and C++ source files using various common file extensions. The extension must recognize all common extensions to avoid missing vtable definitions or implementations in mixed-language projects.

requirements.title: FAIL Macro Failure Handler Navigation
requirements.description: The extension shall provide Go to Definition navigation that resolves failure handler invocations within JUNO_FAIL, JUNO_FAIL_MODULE, JUNO_FAIL_ROOT, and JUNO_ASSERT_EXISTS_MODULE macro call sites to their concrete failure handler implementation functions.
requirements.rationale: LibJuno's FAIL macros invoke failure handlers indirectly through macro expansion. Developers need to trace from the macro call site to the concrete handler function to understand error-handling behavior. The current extension only supports navigation from direct `_pfcnFailureHandler` member access patterns, leaving FAIL macro call sites unresolvable.

requirements.title: JUNO_FAIL Direct Handler Resolution
requirements.description: The extension shall resolve JUNO_FAIL macro call sites by looking up the second argument (the failure handler function pointer) in the function definitions index.
requirements.rationale: JUNO_FAIL takes an explicit function pointer argument. The extension can resolve it directly via the function definitions index without needing derivation chain resolution.

requirements.title: JUNO_FAIL_MODULE Handler Resolution
requirements.description: The extension shall resolve JUNO_FAIL_MODULE macro call sites by determining the type of the second argument (the module pointer), walking the derivation chain to the root type, and looking up the registered failure handler in the failure handler assignments index.
requirements.rationale: JUNO_FAIL_MODULE invokes the handler through `ptMod->JUNO_MODULE_SUPER.JUNO_FAILURE_HANDLER`. The extension must resolve the module pointer type through the derivation chain to find the registered failure handler, consistent with the existing failure handler resolution algorithm.

requirements.title: JUNO_FAIL_ROOT Handler Resolution
requirements.description: The extension shall resolve JUNO_FAIL_ROOT macro call sites by determining the type of the second argument (the module root pointer) and looking up the registered failure handler in the failure handler assignments index.
requirements.rationale: JUNO_FAIL_ROOT invokes the handler through `ptMod->JUNO_FAILURE_HANDLER`. The module pointer is already a root type, so no derivation chain walk is needed — only the failure handler assignment lookup.

requirements.title: JUNO_ASSERT_EXISTS_MODULE Handler Resolution
requirements.description: The extension shall resolve JUNO_ASSERT_EXISTS_MODULE macro call sites by determining the type of the second argument (the module pointer), walking the derivation chain to the root type, and looking up the registered failure handler in the failure handler assignments index.
requirements.rationale: JUNO_ASSERT_EXISTS_MODULE delegates to JUNO_FAIL_MODULE internally. The extension resolves it using the same derivation-chain-based failure handler lookup.

requirements.title: Vtable Resolution Trace View
requirements.description: The extension shall provide a visual trace view that displays the full resolution path from a vtable API call site through the composition root vtable initialization to the concrete implementation function.
requirements.rationale: Developers need to understand not just WHERE a vtable call resolves to, but HOW it resolves — seeing the full chain from call site through the composition root assignment to the implementation. This is especially important for debugging dependency injection configuration issues and understanding the wiring of large LibJuno-based systems.

requirements.title: Trace View Activation via Keyboard
requirements.description: The extension shall activate the vtable resolution trace view when the user presses Ctrl+Shift+T while the cursor is in a C or C++ source file.
requirements.rationale: Ctrl+Shift+T is the dedicated keyboard shortcut for the trace view. Ctrl+Shift+Click cannot be used as it is a built-in VSCode multi-cursor gesture that cannot be overridden.

requirements.title: Trace View Activation via Command Palette
requirements.description: The extension shall provide a Command Palette command to activate the vtable resolution trace view for the vtable API call site at the current cursor position.
requirements.rationale: The Command Palette provides an alternative activation method for users who prefer keyboard-driven workflows or who may have remapped their mouse bindings.

requirements.title: Trace View Call Site Node
requirements.description: The trace view shall display a call site node showing the file path, line number, and the vtable API call expression (e.g., `ptApi->FieldName(...)`).
requirements.rationale: The call site is the starting point of the resolution chain. Displaying the file, line, and call expression provides the developer with immediate context about what is being resolved.

requirements.title: Trace View Composition Root Node
requirements.description: The extension shall display a Composition Root node in the Vtable Trace View showing the call site where the Init function is called to wire the module instance to its concrete vtable. When the caller of the Init function can be resolved, the node links to that call site. When it cannot (e.g., the Init function is called directly from the top-level composition root with no further callers), the node falls back to the initialization implementation location.
requirements.rationale: The composition root is the key piece of the dependency injection chain that connects the abstract call site to the concrete implementation. Showing this assignment helps developers understand and debug the wiring configuration.

requirements.title: Trace View Implementation Node
requirements.description: The trace view shall display an implementation node showing the file path, line number, and the function signature of the concrete implementation function.
requirements.rationale: The implementation function is the final destination of the resolution chain. Displaying the file, line, and signature confirms the resolution result and provides the developer with the information needed to navigate to the implementation.

requirements.title: C11 Expression Grammar Conformance
requirements.description: The extension's C parser shall implement the C11 §6.5 expression grammar such that each unary operator recurses to the correct operand non-terminal: the prefix increment and decrement operators shall recurse to unary-expression, and the unary operators &, *, +, -, ~, and ! shall recurse to cast-expression.
requirements.rationale: Deviation from the C11 unary-expression production causes expressions containing unary operators followed by cast syntax (for example, a dereferenced cast through a function-pointer vtable field) to be parsed incorrectly or not at all. Correct recursion targets are required for the vtable resolver to locate function-pointer call sites reliably, which is a prerequisite for Go-to-Definition navigation.

requirements.title: Ctrl-Hover Preview for Resolvable Symbols
requirements.description: The extension's definition provider shall return at least one resolved target location for every recognized vtable API or failure-handler call site so that VSCode's native Ctrl-hover underline decoration and built-in Go to Definition behavior are available for all resolvable symbols.
requirements.rationale: Users expect Ctrl-hover to visually indicate navigability for every resolvable symbol. Returning at least one resolved location enables the editor's Ctrl-hover underline decoration regardless of how many concrete implementations exist.

requirements.title: Native Peek Window for Multi-Implementation Call Sites
requirements.description: The extension's definition provider shall return all resolved locations for call sites with multiple concrete implementations so that VSCode's native multi-definition peek widget is shown, allowing the user to select the desired implementation before navigating.
requirements.rationale: When a vtable API call site has multiple concrete implementations, the user needs a way to choose the correct target. Returning all resolved locations enables VSCode's built-in multi-definition peek widget, which presents the available implementations for selection in a familiar, zero-learning-curve UI.

requirements.title: Structural Composition Root Detection via API Pointer Call Site
requirements.description: The extension shall identify the composition root of a vtable resolution as the call site where the address of the concrete API struct variable is passed as a function argument (e.g., &gtMyLoggerApi in SomeModule_Init(&tModule, &gtMyLoggerApi, ...)), rather than the vtable struct definition site. This detection shall be structural — based on which variables hold API struct values — and shall not rely on any naming convention of the callee function.
requirements.rationale: LibJuno modules are wired to their concrete vtable implementations via Init functions (e.g., JunoLog_LogInit). The actual runtime composition root is the call site of these functions, not the static definition of the vtable struct. Showing the call site helps developers navigate directly to where the DI wiring occurs.

requirements.title: Trace View Initialization Implementation Node
requirements.description: When the composition root caller is resolved, the extension shall display an Initialization Implementation node in the Vtable Trace View showing the location within the Init function where the module instance is wired to its concrete vtable (e.g., where ptApi = &apiVar is assigned or where &apiVar is passed as a function argument). This node appears between the Composition Root node and the Implementation node.
requirements.rationale: In LibJuno's DI pattern, the API pointer assignment occurs inside the Init function body, not at the user-visible composition root. Surfacing this intermediate wiring step helps developers understand the full initialization chain when debugging DI configuration issues.

requirements.title: Failure Handler Resolution for App-Layer Derived Types
requirements.description: The extension shall resolve failure handler references for module types that derive from any intermediate base type (e.g., JUNO_APP_ROOT_T) that embeds the failure handler field, not only for types that derive directly from JUNO_MODULE_ROOT_T.
requirements.rationale: Modules in the LibJuno app layer derive from JUNO_APP_ROOT_T rather than directly from JUNO_MODULE_ROOT_T. Without this, the entire app layer is invisible to `resolve_failure_handler`, preventing navigation from app-module FAIL macro sites.

requirements.title: Cursor Column Hint in Vtable Not-Found Errors
requirements.description: When no vtable call pattern is found at the specified cursor column, the resolve_vtable_call tool shall scan the source line for the nearest valid vtable call expression and include the suggested column in the error message (e.g., 'No vtable call found at col 27. Nearest match: ->Now at col 62.').
requirements.rationale: Users wasted multiple round-trips guessing the correct column when the cursor was slightly off-target. A scan-and-suggest approach eliminates trial-and-error and was explicitly requested in two separate field reports.

requirements.title: Explicit File-Not-Indexed Error Message
requirements.description: When the queried source file has not been indexed, the resolve_vtable_call tool shall return an error message that explicitly states the file has not been indexed (e.g., 'File not indexed. Ensure the file is within the workspace and the extension has finished indexing.') rather than a generic 'No API type contains field X' message.
requirements.rationale: Users reported that 'No API type contains field Open' implied no type globally had that field, when the actual cause was that the specific file had not been indexed. The misleading message caused confusion about whether the vtable was misnamed or the implementation was missing.

requirements.title: Kind Field in Failure Handler Resolution Results
requirements.description: Each location entry returned by resolve_failure_handler shall include a 'kind' field with value 'assignment' (for sites where the handler is stored into a module root field) or 'invocation' (for JUNO_FAIL_* macro call sites where the handler fires).
requirements.rationale: The current response mixes wiring sites (where the handler is stored) with invocation sites (where JUNO_FAIL_* fires it), making the output ambiguous. Two separate field reports requested this distinction.

requirements.title: Automatic Indexing of Newly Created Files
requirements.description: When a new C or C++ source file with a recognized extension (.c, .h, .cpp, .hpp, .hh, or .cc) is created within the workspace, the extension shall automatically parse and index that file into the navigation index without requiring a manual workspace re-index.
requirements.rationale: Developers create new source files continuously during development. Without automatic indexing of new files, vtable definitions, function implementations, and failure handler assignments in those files remain invisible to the resolver until the user manually triggers a full re-index, producing silent resolution failures on newly written code.

requirements.title: Automatic Index Cleanup on File Deletion
requirements.description: When a C or C++ source file is deleted from the workspace, the extension shall automatically remove all navigation index records associated with that file — including module roots, derivations, vtable assignments, failure handler assignments, function definitions, and local type information — and update the cache to reflect the deletion.
requirements.rationale: Retaining stale index records from deleted files causes the extension to return ghost navigation targets: vtable implementations and function definitions that no longer exist in the codebase. This silently misleads the developer with incorrect Go to Definition results.
